data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-71073116">
    <title>spec: define candidate-cycle review and repair pipeline</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The candidate-cycle review/repair pipeline is not specified with enough precision for independent implementation beads. The current docs say review and repair should happen, but do not define the vocabulary, same-worktree lifecycle, candidate ref retention, read-only reviewer capability, transitional single-slot review allowance, or terminal dispositions.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:337-356 describes adversarial pre-close review and repair outcomes, but it does not state that review should run pre-land in the still-live attempt worktree.
- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md:96-185 defines pre-close review policy, but still allows interpretation as post-land review and does not define read-only tool reviewer mechanics.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:231 mentions pre-close review as a close prerequisite, but does not define candidate-cycle events such as candidate-pinned, repair-cycle-started, approved-land-conflict, or repair-cycle-exhausted.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md:310-354 discusses review evidence assembly, but does not clarify canonical bounded evidence versus supplemental same-worktree read-only access.

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-010 to define the layer-2 candidate-cycle pipeline: create worktree, implementation pass, project-root candidate ref, candidate checks, same-worktree read-only review, repair loop, final land, close/preserve/park, cleanup.
- Update ADR-024 to choose read-only tool reviewer as the review capability model and define same-worktree repair as append-only.
- Update FEAT-022/TD-033 references so bounded evidence remains canonical even when reviewer workdir is the attempt worktree.
- Update TD-031 with candidate-cycle event names, terminal dispositions, budgets, and claim/cooldown behavior.
- Add an explicit transitional allowance for single-slot review until the two-slot bead lands, with a removal condition.

NON-SCOPE
- No Go implementation changes.
- No new CLI flags.
- No manual edits to .ddx/beads.jsonl.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-639af4ef currently covers repair-cycle policy at a high level; this bead supersedes its ambiguous post-land framing by specifying the candidate-cycle pipeline.
- ddx-9228a484 covers retry budgets; this bead names the budget axes so implementation can be decomposed safely.

DEPS
None. ddx-639af4ef and ddx-9228a484 are informational cross-references/intersections, not blocking dependencies for this spec-definition bead.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md defines the candidate, cycle, repair cycle, candidate ref, read-only tool reviewer, and terminal disposition vocabulary.
2. FEAT-010 documents the exact candidate-cycle sequence and states review runs before land in the still-live attempt worktree.
3. docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-024-power-escalation-and-review-routing.md selects read-only tool reviewer as the capability model and states repair commits are append-only with no reset/amend/squash/rebase.
4. docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md defines candidate-pinned, candidate-checks-failed, review-request-changes, repair-cycle-started, approved-land-conflict, repair-cycle-exhausted, review-manual-required, and final-result-landed events.
5. docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-022-prompt-evidence-assembly.md states bounded evidence is canonical and same-worktree access is supplemental/read-only.
6. The docs name repair_max_cycles and review_max_retries_per_candidate as distinct from cost, rate-limit/provider, and no-progress budgets.
7. ddx doc audit exits 0.
8. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run "TestDocAuditCommand" green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:docs, area:specs, kind:doc, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, candidate-cycle</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-31e07fdc" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="839bf94bc5fbc1b513be77d2943eec03dfae1d41" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T060656-387926c5"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All ACs verified. Commit `0b8979c4d` lands the spec for the candidate-cycle review and repair pipeline:

- **FEAT-010**: Added `### Candidate-Cycle Pipeline` section defining all vocabulary (candidate, candidate ref, cycle, repair cycle, read-only tool reviewer, terminal dispositions), the 9-step pre-land sequence, distinct budget axes (`repair_max_cycles`, `review_max_retries_per_candidate`), and transitional single-slot allowance. Updated decision-tree steps 4/5 to reference the pipeline and use the named budget axes.
- **ADR-024**: Selected read-only tool reviewer as the capability model; stated review runs pre-land in still-live worktree; added append-only repair constraint (no reset/amend/squash/rebase); renamed `review_max_retries` → `review_max_retries_per_candidate`; added transitional single-slot allowance; updated Consequences from "no-tool reviewer" → "read-only tool reviewer".
- **TD-031**: Added 7 candidate-cycle events to §4 (`candidate-pinned`, `candidate-checks-failed`, `review-request-changes`, `repair-cycle-started`, `repair-cycle-exhausted`, `approved-land-conflict`, `final-result-landed`).
- **FEAT-022**: Added §2a stating the bounded evidence bundle is canonical and same-worktree access is supplemental/read-only.
- **FEAT-016**: Fixed pre-existing `[[MET-002]]` wiki-link that caused a circular dependency cycle in the doc graph; `ddx doc audit` now exits 0.
